publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-07-24 23:59:27 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Dr. Harry Edwards is a scholar and activist. He organized the Olympic Project for Human Rights, whose most well-known demonstration was the Black Power salute by OHPR members and Olympic medalists John Carlos and Tommie Snith. Additionally, Edwards created the study of the sociology of sport while completing his graduate work at Cornell University, where he completed his PhD in 1970. He went on to teach at the University of California, Berkeley and to advise multiple professional sports organizations. This collection contains documents, buttons, posters, and other memorabilia from his academic career and activism, dating from 1968 through 2020.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection is comprised of memorabilia from Dr. Harry Edwards, dating from 1957-2020. It contains posters, photographs, news clippings, articles by Edwards, notes, buttons, and music CDs from Edwards's activism and academic activities. These include materials on the 1968 Mexico City Olympics, the Olympic Project for Human Rights (OPHR) campaign, athletes John Carlos and Tommie Smith, the Just Stop It campaign against violence against women and girls, support for Black athletes including Colin Kaepernick, and materials relating to President Obama's Super Bowl ring. Edward's holiday greeting cards including one with him taking a knee in front of Trump International Hotel. Though some items are copies, many bear original annotations and/or autographs by Edwards. Other items include materials regarding his FBI file and Foreign Influence in the Black Extremist Movement, some notes relating to Nelson Mandela, and items from Edwards' lectures.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harry Edwards is a civil rights activist and a sociologist whose professional and personal focus centers on the sociology of sport and on athlete activism. Edwards was born in East St. Louis in 1942. After graduating high school in 1960, Edwards attended courses at Fresno City College and then transferred to San Jose State University, where he graduated summa cum laude in 1964 with a Bachelor's degree in sociology. Edwards continued his education in sociology at Cornell University, completing his Master's degree in 1966 and his Ph. D. in 1970. His dissertation launched his academic career as well as establishing the sociology of sport as a new area of study in the field of sociology.

During this time in the late 1960s, Edwards organized the Olympic Project for Human Rights (OPHR), which advocated for human rights and civil rights for Black people as well as protesting racism in athletics. The most well-known demonstration by OPHR members was the Black Power salute by OPHR members and Olympic medalists John Carlos and Tommie Smith (supported by fellow medalist Peter Norman) at the 1968 Summer Olympics in Mexico City.

After completing his degrees, Edwards taught at the University of California, Berkeley starting in 1970 through 2001. He also worked with several different sports organizations including the San Francisco 49ers, Major League Baseball, and the National Basketball Association, serving in roles such as consultant, advisor, counselor, and personnel recruiter, as well as with inmate programs at the San Francisco County Jail and San Quentin State Prison.

Edwards retired from UC Berkeley in 2000, becoming Professor Emeritus of Sociology. He was the director of the Department of Parks and Recreation for the City of Oakland, California from 2001 to 2003. He has continued public and professional engagements, writings, and media appearances. He has also continued his mentorship of Black athletes and athletic professionals.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harry Edwards papers, #8831. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Signed and annotated copies of books by Edwards were part of this donation and have been cataloged individually:
Edwards, Harry. The revolt of the Black athlete. 50th anniversary edition. Urbana: University - of Illinois Press, 2017. [2 copies.]
Edwards, Harry. Sociology of sport. Homewood: Dorsey Press, 1973.
Edwards, Harry. The struggle that must be: An autobiography. New York: MacMillan, 1980.
Journal for the study of sports and athletes in education. 3(2): Summer 2009. Special issue: "The Legacies of Harry Edwards for Sport Sociology."
Ottesen, KK. Activist: Portraits of courage. San Francisco: Chronicle Books, 2019. [This book is signed and annotated on the first page of Dr. Edwards's section, p. 252.]
Polite, Fritz G. and Billy Hawkins. Sport, race, activism, and social change: The impact of Dr. Harry Edwards' scholarship and service. San Diego: Cognella, 2012.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Challenge to Governance Oral History Project, #13=6=1285. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library. https://rmc.library.cornell.edu/EAD/htmldocs/RMA01285.html

Conversation with Harry Edwards Audio-Visual Materials #/4017 AV. Kheel Center for Labor-Management Documentation and Archives. Cornell University Library. https://rmc.library.cornell.edu/EAD/htmldocs/KCL04017av.html
Harry Edwards Collection at San Jose State Universitiy. https://www.sjsu.edu/wordstoaction/legacies/edwards/collection.php
Harry Edwards: An Oral History. https://digicoll.lib.berkeley.edu/record/103583 University of California, Berkeley: 2005.

Civil rights.
Domestic Violence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original political buttons from the Olympic Project for Human Rights and the Just Stop It campaign against violence against women and girls.

did.unittitle: Signed CDs of "San Jose Suite" by Etienne Charles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Audio CDs of "San Jose Suite" by Etienne Charles, signed by both Charles and Harry Edwards.

did.unittitle: Materials on President Barack Obama's Super Bowl ring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, images, notes, and articles on Harry Edwards' gift of his Super Bowl ring to President Barack Obama in 2014.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original uncorrected draft of "Paradigm Shift: Partnering in Elevating the Status, Circumstances, and Outcomes of Women and Girls." Presentation given by Dr. Harry Edwards at the University of Texas, Austin, April 7-9, 2015.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holiday greeting cards with photographs of Dr. Harry Edwards, signed and dated by Edwards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from Dr. Harry Edwards interspersed with images and articles that have been scanned and printed out, many of which are also annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sports section of Bay Area News Group from June 3, 2020, featuring Colin Kaepernick. Annotated by Edwards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images of correspondence and photographs that have been scanned and printed out, along with event posters, most annotated and/or signed by Edwards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic portrait of Dr. Harry Edwards, mounted on foamcore with glass cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster calling for African American boycott of 1968 Olympic Games. Includes note from Edwards on Nelson Mandela having a copy of this poster in his cell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images (one blown up to large size) of FBI information on Harry Thomas Edwards, including his photograph.

did.unittitle: Materials on Jackie Robinson and Martin Luther King Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quote and image of Jackie Robinson and image of Jackie Robinson with Martin Luther King Jr, with quote by Harry Edwards, annotated and signed by Edwards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles and scanned images and photographs, blown up to large sizes, interspersed with event posters and political posters. Most are signed and/or annotated by Edwards.
